# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch snapshot configuration for automated backups. Configure snapshot repository to S3 or file share for the operations team. Implement snapshot creation and restoration functionality.
# Category: datastore_security

```yaml
es_snapshot_settings: 
    repositories:
        myRepositoryName: # Name of your Elasticsearch Snapshots Repository   (Required)      
            type: 's3'     # Type can be either file or s3.                   (Default is S3, Required if you want to use external storage like AWS EBS/SFS etc.) 
            
        myRepositoryName_snapshotRepo1name_:   # Repository name                     (Required)      
            type: 'fs'     # Type can be either file or s3.                   (Default is FS, Required if you want to use local disk for snapshot repo like in-memory DB etc.) 
            
        myRepositoryName_snapshotRepo2name_:   # Repository name                     (Required)      
            type: 'fs'     # Type can be either file or s3.                   (Default is FS, Required if you want to use local disk for snapshot repo like in-memory DB etc.) 
            
    snapshots_settings:        # Configuration about Elasticsearch Snapshot              ()     
        10MBSnapName_:   # Name of the repository          (Required)       -> Naming Convention is "<repository name><snapshot type and size description ex. s3a_dailyBackup>"     you can use any alphanumeric string here like 's2abcd'
            repositories:  ["myRepositoryName"]   # Name of the repository          (Required)       ->  This is a list containing all Snapshot Repositorie names. All snapshots will be created under these repos    ex["repo1name","repo_snapRepo"]. If any repo missing, error can occur
            trigger: 50mb # Size of the snapshot repository in bytes (required)   ->  When size reaches or exceeds this limit triggers a Snapshot. Ex : Trigger value is set to '2gb' which means after setting up and running it for exactly two GBs only then backup would be triggered
            
            name: "snapName"    # Name of the snapshot  (Required)       -> Unique string that identifies this repository   ex. s3a_dailyBackup     or 'abcd'
              wait_for_completion : true      # Indicates if snapshots need to be completed before restoring is allowed                  ()    default value: false  (Optional)       ->  This option will ensure the snapshot operation finishes successfully then it can restore. Set this flag as required ex[wait_for_Completions=true]
            timeout : "10m"     # Timeout for operations in case snapshots are not completed          ()    default value: '6h' (Optional)      ->  If the snapshot operation is taking longer than specified time then it should be stopped. Ex:'5d', It will fail if a backup took more 
                                    duration to complete and could result into data loss   ex[timeout="30m"]   `       # The above are examples of timeout options you can define as per requirement, in format 'xh' where x is number or minutes. Default value: "6h"(Optional)        ->  This option will tell Elasticsearch to stop the backup after certain time and also fail if snapshot operation
                                            not completed within specified duration  ex [timeout="30m"] `   # And above example can be implemented as per requirement, in format 'xh' where x is number or minutes. Default value: "6h"(Optional)    ->  This option will tell Elasticsearch to stop the backup after certain time and also fail if snapshot operation
                                            not completed within specified duration  ex [timeout="30m"] `   # And above example can be implemented as per requirement, in format 'xh' where x is number or minutes.                                             not completed within specified duration  ex [timeout="30m"] `   # And above example can be implemented as per requirement, in format 'xh' where x is number or minutes.                                             not completed within specified duration  ex [timeout="30m"] `   # And above example can be implemented as per requirement, in format 'xh' where x is number or minutes. Default value: "6h"(Optional)    ->  This option will tell Elasticsearch to stop the backup after certain time and also fail if snapshot operation
                                            not completed within specified duration  ex [timeout="30m"] `   # And above example can be implemented as per requirement, in format 'xh' where x is number or minutes. Default value: "6h"(Optional)    ->  This option will tell Elasticsearch to stop the backup after certain time and also fail if snapshot operation
                                            not completed within specified duration  ex [timeout="30m"] `   # And above example can be implemented as per requirement, in format 'xh' where x is number or minutes. Default value: "6h"(Optional)    ->  This option will tell Elasticsearch to stop the backup after certain time and also fail if snapshot operation
                                            not completed within specified duration  ex [timeout="30m"] `   # And above example can be implemented as per requirement, in format 'xh' where x is number or minutes. Default value: "6h"(Optional)    ->  This option will tell Elasticsearch to stop the backup after certain time and also fail if snapshot operation
                                            not completed within specified duration  ex [timeout="30m"] `   # And above example can be implemented as per requirement, in format 'xh' where x is number or minutes. Default value: "6h"(Optional)
